 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rbke","title":"E2E Tests: Help Center & Support","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-xwg2.

## Task
Create E2E tests for help center and support functionality.

## Test Scenarios

### Help Center
1. Help center page loads
2. FAQ categories display
3. Search within help works
4. Article pages load
5. Related articles shown
6. Contact support CTA visible

### Contact Form
7. Form displays correctly
8. Required fields validated
9. Category selection works
10. Submission succeeds
11. Confirmation shown
12. Email sent to support

### Ticket System
13. User can view tickets
14. Ticket history displays
15. Can reply to open ticket
16. Status updates shown

### Search
17. Help search returns results
18. No results shows suggestions
19. Search is fast

## Files to Create
- e2e/support/help-center.spec.ts
- e2e/support/contact-form.spec.ts
- e2e/support/tickets.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/support-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- Help center fully tested
- Contact form works
- User journey complete

## Logging Requirements
- Log search queries and results count
- Log contact form payload validation errors
- Capture screenshots for submission success and failure

## UX Improvements
- Ensure help search returns friendly suggestions when empty
- Contact CTA visible on every help article page

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rd99","title":"Document CLI-Premium integration architecture","description":"## Task
Document the integration architecture between the free CLI (jfp) and the premium SaaS backend.

## Documentation Sections

### 1. Authentication Flow
- Device code flow (RFC 8628) explanation
- Token storage and refresh
- Error handling

### 2. API Endpoints
- /api/cli/device-code - Initiate auth
- /api/cli/token - Exchange code for token
- /api/cli/sync - Sync library
- /api/cli/collections - Manage collections

### 3. Local Cache Structure
- ~/.jeffreysprompts/ directory layout
- credentials.json format
- library/ cache structure
- Sync metadata

### 4. Prompt Format Compatibility
- Free tier prompt format (TypeScript)
- Premium prompt format (database)
- Conversion between formats
- SKILL.md export format

### 5. Offline Mode
- What works offline
- Cache invalidation
- Conflict resolution

## Files to Create
- docs/cli-premium-integration.md
- docs/api-reference.md
- Update README.md with premium features section

## Success Criteria
- Clear documentation for contributors
- API contracts documented
- Cache format specified
- Troubleshooting guide included

## Cross-Repo Integration
- Premium device code flow: jeffreysprompts_premium-0o1
- Premium verification page: jeffreysprompts_premium-qdm
- Premium CLI auth page: jeffreysprompts_premium-mn0
- Premium CLI JWT/token generation: jeffreysprompts_premium-qnl
- Premium token refresh/revoke: jeffreysprompts_premium-0ve
- Premium CLI sync endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-67o","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T05:31:23.094413840Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T17:14:05.791773776Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T17:14:05.791773776Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive CLI-Premium integration docs (cli-premium-integration.md + api-reference.md)","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rd99","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-akwo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T07:54:20.526620963Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rd99","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-bojj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T18:04:36.038383066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rd99","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-hiff","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T07:45:12.029126352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rd99","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-uw7z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T07:44:52.838533775Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rd99","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wlg3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T07:45:29.926171438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rd99","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xzgq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T07:45:45.072648583Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rhvk","title":"E2E Tests: Network Resilience and Offline Mode","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-2b44. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Create E2E tests for network failure handling and offline mode.

## Why This Matters
- Users have bad connections
- Mobile users go offline
- Must handle gracefully
- No data loss

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Network Failure Handling
```typescript
test('should show error message on network failure', async ({ page, context }) => {
  await page.goto('/dashboard');
  
  // Simulate offline
  await context.setOffline(true);
  
  // Try to load more content
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Load More' }).click();
  
  // Should show offline message
  await expect(page.getByText(/network.*error|offline/i)).toBeVisible();
});

test('should retry failed requests', async ({ page, context }) => {
  await page.goto('/dashboard');
  
  // Intercept and fail first request
  let requestCount = 0;
  await page.route('**/api/prompts**', async (route) => {
    requestCount++;
    if (requestCount === 1) {
      await route.abort('connectionfailed');
    } else {
      await route.continue();
    }
  });
  
  // Should auto-retry and succeed
  await page.reload();
  await expect(page.getByTestId('prompt-list')).toBeVisible();
  expect(requestCount).toBe(2);
});
```

### 2. Offline Indicator
```typescript
test('should show offline indicator when disconnected', async ({ page, context }) => {
  await page.goto('/dashboard');
  
  await context.setOffline(true);
  
  // Should show offline indicator
  await expect(page.getByTestId('offline-indicator')).toBeVisible();
  
  await context.setOffline(false);
  
  // Should hide indicator
  await expect(page.getByTestId('offline-indicator')).not.toBeVisible();
});
```

### 3. Offline Data Access (PWA)
```typescript
test('should serve cached prompts when offline', async ({ page, context }) => {
  // First, visit while online to cache
  await page.goto('/prompts/test-prompt-id');
  await expect(page.getByTestId('prompt-content')).toBeVisible();
  
  // Go offline
  await context.setOffline(true);
  
  // Should still show cached content
  await page.reload();
  await expect(page.getByTestId('prompt-content')).toBeVisible();
  await expect(page.getByText(/offline.*cached/i)).toBeVisible();
});

test('should queue actions when offline', async ({ page, context }) => {
  await page.goto('/prompts/test-prompt-id/edit');
  
  await context.setOffline(true);
  
  // Make an edit
  await page.getByLabel('Title').fill('Updated Title');
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Save' }).click();
  
  // Should show "queued" message
  await expect(page.getByText(/saved.*sync/i)).toBeVisible();
  
  // Go back online
  await context.setOffline(false);
  
  // Should sync automatically
  await expect(page.getByText(/synced/i)).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10000 });
});
```

### 4. Slow Network Handling
```typescript
test('should show loading states on slow network', async ({ page }) => {
  // Simulate slow network
  const client = await page.context().newCDPSession(page);
  await client.send('Network.emulateNetworkConditions', {
    offline: false,
    downloadThroughput: 50 * 1024, // 50kb/s
    uploadThroughput: 50 * 1024,
    latency: 2000, // 2s latency
  });
  
  await page.goto('/search');
  await page.getByPlaceholder('Search').fill('test');
  await page.keyboard.press('Enter');
  
  // Should show loading skeleton
  await expect(page.getByTestId('search-skeleton')).toBeVisible();
});

test('should timeout and show message on very slow network', async ({ page }) => {
  // Simulate extremely slow network
  await page.route('**/api/**', async (route) => {
    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 30000));
    await route.continue();
  });
  
  await page.goto('/dashboard');
  
  // Should show timeout message
  await expect(page.getByText(/taking.*long|timeout/i)).toBeVisible({ timeout: 15000 });
});
```

### 5. Reconnection Handling
```typescript
test('should automatically reconnect and refresh', async ({ page, context }) => {
  await page.goto('/dashboard');
  
  await context.setOffline(true);
  await page.waitForTimeout(1000);
  await context.setOffline(false);
  
  // Should show reconnection message
  await expect(page.getByText(/back online|reconnected/i)).toBeVisible();
  
  // Should refresh data
  await expect(page.getByTestId('last-updated')).toContainText(/just now/i);
});
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log network state changes
- Log retry attempts
- Log cache hits/misses
- Screenshot error states

## Files to Create
- e2e/network/failures.spec.ts
- e2e/network/offline.spec.ts
- e2e/network/slow.spec.ts
- e2e/network/reconnection.spec.ts

## Success Criteria
- Errors shown clearly
- Retries work
- Offline mode functions
- Reconnection smooth

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ri69","title":"Fix touch target sizes to meet 44px minimum","description":"# Task: Fix Touch Target Sizes

## Context
Apple and Google HIG recommend minimum 44x44px touch targets for comfortable interaction. Several components violate this guideline.

## Specific Violations

### 1. SpotlightSearch Category Pills (lines 517-543)
**Current:** `py-1.5` ≈ 28px height
```tsx
<button className="shrink-0 px-3 py-1.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium ...">
```

**Fix:** Increase to `py-2.5` for ~40px, or add min-height
```tsx
<button className="shrink-0 px-3 py-2.5 min-h-[44px] rounded-full text-sm font-medium ...">
```

### 2. BasketSidebar Remove Button (lines 223-229)
**Current:** `h-7 w-7` = 28px
```tsx
<Button variant="ghost" size="icon" className="h-7 w-7 shrink-0">
  <X className="h-3 w-3" />
</Button>
```

**Fix:** Increase to `h-9 w-9` = 36px (or h-11 w-11 = 44px)
```tsx
<Button 
  variant="ghost" 
  size="icon" 
  className="h-9 w-9 shrink-0 -mr-2" // Negative margin to maintain visual spacing
  aria-label={`Remove ${prompt.title} from basket`}
>
  <X className="h-4 w-4" />
</Button>
```

### 3. SpotlightSearch Close Button (mobile, lines 497-504)
**Current:** `size-8` = 32px
```tsx
<button className="shrink-0 size-8 rounded-lg ...">
```

**Fix:** Already close, bump to `size-10` or `size-11`
```tsx
<button className="shrink-0 size-11 rounded-lg ...">
```

### 4. SpotlightSearch Semantic Toggle (lines 480-494)
**Current:** `size-8` = 32px
**Fix:** Bump to `size-10` for touch friendliness

## Pattern for Touch-Friendly Buttons
```tsx
// Utility class for consistent touch targets
// Could add to globals.css:
.touch-target {
  @apply min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px];
}

// Or use Tailwind directly:
className="min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px] ..."
```

## Design Considerations
- Larger touch targets may require layout adjustments
- Use negative margins to maintain visual density if needed
- Icon size should scale proportionally (but not required)
- Consider adding `touch-manipulation` class for faster response

## Visual Impact
- SpotlightSearch category pills will be slightly taller
- Basket remove buttons will have more breathing room
- Overall feel: more native/mobile-friendly

## Testing
1. Test on actual mobile device (simulator isn't accurate for touch)
2. Verify tap accuracy on category pills
3. Check remove button in basket with full list
4. Ensure no layout overflow issues
5. Compare before/after screenshots

## Files Modified
- apps/web/src/components/SpotlightSearch.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/BasketSidebar.tsx

## Dependencies
- Independent of color changes
- May affect overall mobile layout slightly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:40:03.618658128Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T18:20:08.611045100Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T18:20:08.611045100Z","close_reason":"Fixed touch target sizes: SpotlightSearch category pills, semantic toggle, mobile close button; BasketSidebar remove button. All now meet 44px minimum. Build passes.","compaction_level":0}

- Privacy signals respected

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xgh","title":"Create static registry routes (/registry.json, /registry.manifest.json)","description":"**Details:**
- Static JSON files generated at build time
- /registry.json - full prompt registry with all metadata
- /registry.manifest.json - checksums for cache validation

**/registry.json Format:**
```json
{
  "schemaVersion": 1,
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "updatedAt": "2026-01-10T00:00:00Z",
  "prompts": [...],
  "bundles": [...],
  "workflows": [...],
  "meta": {
    "totalPromptCount": 50,
    "categories": ["ideation", ...],
    "tags": ["ultrathink", ...]
  }
}
```

**/registry.manifest.json Format:**
```json
{
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "generatedAt": "2026-01-10T00:00:00Z",
  "checksums": {
    "registry": "sha256:abc123...",
    "prompts": {
      "idea-wizard": "sha256:def456...",
      ...
    }
  }
}
```

**Build Script Integration:**
- scripts/build-data.ts generates both files
- Files placed in apps/web/public/
- ETag headers based on manifest checksums
- CLI uses manifest for cache validation

**Location:** 
- apps/web/public/registry.json (generated)
- apps/web/public/registry.manifest.json (generated)

**Reasoning:** Static registry files enable CLI caching, offline PWA access, and efficient client-side search without API calls.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:34:33.677563084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T07:39:27.800646247Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T07:39:27.800646247Z","close_reason":"Registry files already generated: registry.json and registry.manifest.json exist with proper schema version, checksums, and prompts data.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xgh","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-n3p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:37:57.215409515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xjfe","title":"Implement trending and discovery algorithm","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-8f3t.

## Task
Create algorithm for surfacing trending and recommended content.

## Why This Matters
- Help users discover great content
- Reward quality contributors
- Drive engagement
- Fresh content on homepage

## Trending Signals

### Engagement Metrics
- Views (weighted by recency)
- Saves/bookmarks
- Uses (copied/exported)
- Shares
- Ratings (weighted average)

### Quality Signals
- Creator reputation
- Content completeness
- Has description
- Has examples
- Tagged appropriately

### Freshness
- Recent content boosted
- Decay over time
- Evergreen content exceptions

## Algorithm

### Trending Score
```typescript
function calculateTrendingScore(prompt: Prompt, window: Duration): number {
  const viewScore = weightedViews(prompt.views, window) * 1.0;
  const saveScore = prompt.saveCount * 3.0;
  const useScore = prompt.useCount * 2.0;
  const shareScore = prompt.shareCount * 2.5;
  const ratingScore = (prompt.avgRating - 3) * prompt.ratingCount * 0.5;
  
  const engagementScore = viewScore + saveScore + useScore + shareScore + ratingScore;
  
  // Quality multiplier (0.5 - 1.5)
  const qualityMultiplier = calculateQualityScore(prompt);
  
  // Freshness decay
  const freshnessDecay = Math.pow(0.95, daysSinceCreated(prompt));
  
  return engagementScore * qualityMultiplier * freshnessDecay;
}
```

### Personalized Recommendations
- Based on user's saved content
- Based on viewing history
- Similar to liked items
- From followed creators
- Collaborative filtering (users like you)

## Implementation

### Batch Calculation
- Run hourly for trending
- Cache results
- Incremental updates

### Database
```typescript
export const trendingPrompts = pgTable('trending_prompts', {
  promptId: uuid('prompt_id').references(() => userPrompts.id).primaryKey(),
  score: numeric('score').notNull(),
  rank: integer('rank'),
  period: text('period'), // daily, weekly, monthly
  calculatedAt: timestamp('calculated_at').defaultNow(),
});
```

## Discovery Pages
- /explore - Browse trending
- /explore/new - Recent high-quality
- /explore/top - All-time best
- /for-you - Personalized

## Files to Create
- src/lib/discovery/trending.ts
- src/lib/discovery/recommendations.ts
- src/app/api/cron/calculate-trending/route.ts
- src/app/(app)/explore/page.tsx

## Success Criteria
- Trending reflects actual engagement
- Fresh content surfaces
- Quality content rewarded
- Personalization improves discovery

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xzgq","title":"Implement CLI offline mode and auto-update","description":"## Task
Add offline caching and auto-update mechanism to the jfp CLI.

## Why This Matters
- CLI users work offline sometimes
- Need access to synced prompts
- Auto-update keeps CLI current
- Better developer experience

## Scope
This is CLI client-side behavior and belongs in the public repo.
Depends on Premium API sync endpoints from `jeffreysprompts_premium-67o`.

## Features

### 1. Local Cache
- Cache synced prompts locally
- Cache authentication tokens
- Cache user preferences
- SQLite or JSON file storage

### 2. Offline Mode
- Detect network unavailable
- Serve from local cache
- Queue sync operations
- Show offline indicator

### 3. Auto-Update
- Check for updates on launch
- Download in background
- Prompt to restart
- Self-update mechanism

### 4. Configuration
- Cache directory location
- Cache size limits
- Update channel (stable/beta)
- Offline mode toggle

## Implementation

### Local Cache (CLI)
```ts
// ~/.jeffreysprompts/cache/
interface LocalCache {
  prompts: Map<string, CachedPrompt>;
  collections: Map<string, CachedCollection>;
  lastSync: Date;
  version: string;
}

class CacheManager {
  private cacheDir: string;

  async cachePrompt(prompt: Prompt): Promise<void> {
    const path = join(this.cacheDir, 'prompts', `${prompt.id}.json`);
    await writeFile(path, JSON.stringify(prompt));
  }

  async getPrompt(id: string): Promise<Prompt | null> {
    const path = join(this.cacheDir, 'prompts', `${id}.json`);
    if (!existsSync(path)) return null;
    return JSON.parse(await readFile(path, 'utf-8'));
  }

  async sync(): Promise<SyncResult> {
    const lastSync = await this.getLastSync();
    const changes = await api.getChangesSince(lastSync);
    // Apply changes to cache
    return { added: changes.added.length, updated: changes.updated.length };
  }
}
```

### Offline Detection
```ts
async function withOfflineSupport<T>(
  onlineAction: () => Promise<T>,
  offlineAction: () => Promise<T>
): Promise<T> {
  try {
    return await onlineAction();
  } catch (error) {
    if (isNetworkError(error)) {
      console.log(chalk.yellow('Offline mode: using cached data'));
      return offlineAction();
    }
    throw error;
  }
}
```

### Auto-Update
```ts
async function checkForUpdates(): Promise<UpdateInfo | null> {
  const currentVersion = getPackageVersion();
  const latestVersion = await fetchLatestVersion();

  if (semver.gt(latestVersion, currentVersion)) {
    return {
      current: currentVersion,
      latest: latestVersion,
      releaseNotes: await fetchReleaseNotes(latestVersion),
      downloadUrl: getDownloadUrl(latestVersion),
    };
  }
  return null;
}

async function performUpdate(info: UpdateInfo): Promise<void> {
  const spinner = ora('Downloading update...').start();

  // Download new version
  const binary = await downloadBinary(info.downloadUrl);

  // Replace current binary
  await replaceBinary(binary);

  spinner.succeed(`Updated to v${info.latest}`);
  console.log('Please restart the CLI to use the new version.');
}
```

### Config File
```yaml
# ~/.jeffreysprompts/config.yaml
cache:
  enabled: true
  maxSize: 100MB
  location: ~/.jeffreysprompts/cache

updates:
  channel: stable  # stable, beta
  autoCheck: true
  autoInstall: false

offline:
  enabled: true
  queueSync: true
```

## Files to Create (in CLI repo)
- src/cache/cache-manager.ts
- src/cache/offline-mode.ts
- src/updates/update-checker.ts
- src/updates/self-update.ts
- Use config module from jeffreysprompts.com-ctt5 (do not reimplement config manager)

## API Additions (Premium repo)
- GET /api/cli/sync?since=timestamp
- GET /api/cli/version (latest version info)

## Logging Requirements
- Log offline/online detection decisions and retry/backoff
- Log cache directory used and cache size accounting
- Log update checks (current vs latest) and update path chosen
- Log sync queue size and flush attempts when back online

## Success Criteria
- Prompts accessible offline
- Changes queued and synced
- Auto-update notifies users
- Config customizable

## Origin
Moved from `jeffreysprompts_premium-e2oq`.

## Cross-Repo Integration
- Premium CLI sync endpoints for offline cache refresh: jeffreysprompts_premium-67o","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-11T07:23:28.007456522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T14:45:26.456228052Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T14:45:26.456228052Z","close_reason":"Implemented offline mode with network detection, cached data fallback for search/list commands, and auto-update check on CLI launch","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xzgq","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ctt5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T07:25:14.958174135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xzgq","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-hiff","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T07:23:28.015832600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xzgq","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-uw7z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T07:23:28.013113905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
